judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully hit all four expectations with commit-grounded evidence; they are comparable, with Report 2 slightly more thorough on remediation details but neither better on the graded expectations.

report_1.evidence: these exact steps were already implemented and shipped, then reverted in production — so step 0 is: do not treat this as greenfield.

report_2.evidence: This migration already failed in production and was reverted (PLAT-31).** Commit `1712565` (2026-04-21)

report_1.evidence: Commit `1712565` is titled *"Revert Edgerunner migration: serverless isolates cannot hold the Postgres pool; /reports/monthly exhausted Postgres max_connections in production, back to Cargobox container deploy (PLAT-31)."* Its body: *"Under normal dashboard load the per-request connections opened faster than Postgres could recycle them and tripped the connection limit ('FATAL: too many connections')

report_2.evidence: Commit `1712565` (2026-04-21) message: *"Revert Edgerunner migration: serverless isolates cannot hold the Postgres pool; /reports/monthly exhausted Postgres max_connections in production… Under normal dashboard load the per-request connections opened faster than Postgres could recycle them and tripped the connection limit ('FATAL: too many connections'), taking the endpoint down."*

report_1.evidence: The identical migration was implemented, run in production, caused a customer-facing outage (`/reports/monthly` down with `FATAL: too many connections`), and was deliberately reverted roughly three months ago (revert `1712565`, dated Apr 21 2026

report_2.evidence: this exact migration was built and then reverted. Read `git show 1712565` before doing anything else.

report_1.evidence: this is explicitly not an engineer's call to make silently. Bring the prior failure (commit `1712565`, ticket PLAT-31) to: the platform/infra owner behind the PLAT-N tickets (system owner)

report_2.evidence: Loop in (a) the **Platform team** that owns the PLAT tickets / PLAT-31 (they ran and reverted the prior attempt and hold the post-mortem)... The decision should be made jointly and explicitly informed by the PLAT-31 history.
